---
title: "Inputs"
---

Propagate uses two inputs to generate the outputs described on the
[Outputs](/docs/design/interface/outputs.qmd) page. See the
[Design overview page](/docs/design/index.qmd) for a description of the user types,
Requester and Owner, referred to below.

The inputs include:

- A structured metadata file for the data package, for example the `datapackage.json` from the
  [Data Package](https://specs.frictionlessdata.io/data-package/) specification.
  Owners use this file to generate a web component through which a Requester can
  create a data request.

<!-- TODO: Add `data-dict.yaml` as well? -->

- A data request, in a `request.toml` file, made through the web component or terminal interface by a
  Requester. The request file includes a SQL-like query of selected resources,
  fields, and filters, as well as any additional metadata about the request
  (e.g., requester name, email, project name, and rationale). The Owner uses
  this file to create the subset of data from the data package.

<!-- TODO: Agree on what word(s) to use about the data request/subset/instructions form and align across design docs -->

<!-- TODO: Do we want to add a config so users can customise the content of the web component, as we in Flower? -->
